coach_note: Diagnosed with tonsillitis — confirmed by doctor on 24 Mar. Declining antibiotics as symptoms are improving. No running since Thu 19 Mar (5 days). Resting HR stable at 41 bpm which is encouraging. Plan: complete rest through the week, aiming to return with an easy test run Sat 28 or Sun 29 Mar. If that goes well, first quality session (5×2k at 5:35/mi) lands midweek of 31 Mar. One week lost from the 7-week Bristol build — manageable given the deep aerobic base from consistent 55-60 mi/wk training. CTL will dip further this week but will rebound quickly once volume returns. No panic.

predictions.rationale: Paddock Wood 1:17:34 on a hilly course as a practice race. One week lost to tonsillitis is absorbed by the existing fitness base. 6 weeks of specific work remain — enough for the key race-pace sessions (5×2k, 6×2k, tempos). Wider range reflects illness uncertainty. If return to training goes smoothly by end of this week, the target stays realistic. Bristol is flat and fast.

PB 16:11 set during marathon training with no 5K-specific work. HM build is strengthening the aerobic engine. Burgess parkrun 16:59 in Feb on a hilly course, untapered. Summer 5K block with specific speedwork on top of this base should deliver sub-16. Illness doesn't materially affect this longer-term goal.
